description: Paracetamol is an analgesic and antipyretic. It is also known as acetaminophen and is commonly sold under brand names such as Panadol, Tylenol, and Calpol.
Ibuprofen is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) used for pain, fever, and inflammation. It is marketed under brand names such as Advil, Motrin, Nurofen, and Brufen.
Aspirin is also known as acetylsalicylic acid and appears under brand names such as Bayer Aspirin, Ecotrin, and Bufferin. Low-dose aspirin may also be referred to as baby aspirin.
Warfarin is an anticoagulant used to prevent and treat blood clots. It is marketed under brand names such as Coumadin and Jantoven.
Amoxicillin is a penicillin-class antibiotic. It is commonly sold under brand names such as Amoxil and Moxatag.
Augmentin is a combination product containing amoxicillin and clavulanic acid. This entry is important for normalization of combination products.
Metformin is an oral antihyperglycemic medicine used for type 2 diabetes. It appears under brand names such as Glucophage, Fortamet, and Glumetza.
Atorvastatin is a statin used to lower cholesterol. It is commonly sold under the brand name Lipitor.
Omeprazole is a proton pump inhibitor used for acid-related disorders. It is marketed under brand names such as Prilosec and Losec.
Naproxen is an NSAID used for pain and inflammation. It is marketed under brand names such as Aleve and Naprosyn.
Lisinopril is an ACE inhibitor used to treat hypertension and heart failure. It is sold under brand names such as Prinivil and Zestril.
Azithromycin is a macrolide antibiotic marketed under the brand name Zithromax.
Prednisone is a corticosteroid used for inflammatory and autoimmune conditions. It is marketed under brand names such as Deltasone and Rayos.
Ciprofloxacin is a fluoroquinolone antibiotic commonly marketed as Cipro.
Losartan is an angiotensin II receptor blocker used to treat hypertension and is sold under the brand name Cozaar.
Albuterol is a bronchodilator used for asthma and COPD and appears under brand names such as Ventolin and ProAir.
Clopidogrel is an antiplatelet medicine marketed under the brand name Plavix.
Hydrochlorothiazide is a thiazide diuretic marketed under brand names such as Microzide. It is also commonly abbreviated as HCTZ.
Simvastatin is a statin medicine used to lower cholesterol and is sold under the brand name Zocor.
Codeine is an opioid analgesic and antitussive. It is often present in combination products, especially with paracetamol or acetaminophen.
Doxycycline is a tetracycline antibiotic commonly marketed under the brand name Vibramycin.
Metoclopramide is included as a normalization entry because it appears directly in the interaction test cases with paracetamol.
Cholestyramine is included as a normalization entry because it appears in test cases involving paracetamol absorption interactions.
Lamotrigine is included as a normalization entry because it appears in test cases involving ingredient-level interaction checks with paracetamol.
Isoniazid is included as a normalization entry because it appears in test cases related to liver-toxicity risk with paracetamol.
Zidovudine is included as a normalization entry because it appears in test cases involving paracetamol interaction retrieval.
Flucloxacillin is included as a normalization entry because it appears in edge-case test cases with paracetamol.
Clarithromycin is included as a normalization entry because it appears in test cases involving atorvastatin interaction checks.
Metronidazole is included as a normalization entry to support parsing of formulation-specific test cases such as metronidazole injection.
Sodium chloride is included as a normalization entry to support safe parsing of non-oral formulation names such as 0.9% sodium chloride irrigation.
Oral contraceptives are included as a class-like normalization target because they appear in test queries and may not always be specified by exact product name.
Herbal supplement is included as an uncertain class-level input to support safe handling of underspecified test queries.
